# graphobs v0.2.0

Safer migration ergonomics for existing LangGraph apps.

## Highlights

- Adds callback payload projection for non-invasive trace curation.
- Adds optional callback match diagnostics for observed, matched, unmatched, and
  missing LangGraph node names.
- Adds contract discovery helpers for synthetic sample states.
- Adds pass-through node execution and read auditing guardrails for existing
  nodes that still need full graph state during migration.
- Narrows package-root exports to the headline interface while keeping
  lower-level primitives available from focused submodules.
- Ships `py.typed` so downstream type checkers can treat the package as typed.

## Migration Notes

For existing graphs, start with `project_callback_payloads` when you only need
cleaner callback payloads. Use `contract_node(..., pass_through_state=True,
audit_reads=True)` when you want read/write contract warnings without filtering
the state passed to the node. Use default `contract_node` behavior once the node
is ready for strict projected execution.
